meta.blockSubtitle: Municipal Survey Authority · Block 01 · Survey Assignment 7734-C
meta.worldContract: The world is larger on the inside than the outside, and the institution that sent you here already knew that.

narrativeVoice.narratorStance: You are a surveyor. The building does not behave like a building.
narrativeVoice.voiceRationale: Second person present creates a direct, embodied connection between the lifting effort and the surveyor's physical experience. The reader is not observing — they are doing the survey. This mirrors the act of logging a workout: both are records of physical work performed.

literaryRegister.name: Bureaucratic Sublime
literaryRegister.behaviorDescription: Official language deployed with total confidence against material that has no official explanation. The forms continue to be filed. The procedures continue to be followed. The building continues to be larger on the inside than geometry allows. Neither fact cancels the other.
forbiddenMoves: Characters who react with visible fear or panic — the register is one of professional persistence
Explicit horror framing — the uncanny must arrive through bureaucratic matter-of-factness
Explanations — the world does not explain itself and neither does the prose
literaryRegister.typographicBehavior: Week 1: stable, authoritative, every form field filled. The grid is functioning. By Week 5 the same typefaces will feel like they are working too hard to hold a shape they have already lost.

structuralShape.promptFragmentRelationship: parallel — session prompts and fragments run alongside each other; neither contradicts; the fragments complicate what the prompts take for granted
structuralShape.shapeRationale: Block 1 refuses resolution — the survey report is filed but the building is never explained. The narrator is reliable in Week 1 and remains technically reliable throughout, but the reader's trust in the narrator's framework erodes as the framework fails to account for what the building is doing.

meta.liftoScript:
## Cartographer Block 01
## 3 days/week · Linear Progression
prog squat / lp / 5 / 2.5
prog bench / lp / 5 / 2.5
prog deadlift / lp / 5 / 5

week
  day A
    squat / 3x5 / 135
    bench / 3x5 / 95
    barbell row / 3x5 / 95
  day B
    squat / 3x5 / 137.5
    ohp / 3x5 / 65
    deadlift / 1x5 / 185

epigraph.text: The building at the address provided does not appear in municipal records. Proceed with survey as assigned.
epigraph.attribution: MSA Dispatch Memo 7734 · Re: Assignment C · Date of issue: redacted

weeklyComponent.extractionInstruction: Decode the sequence using the substitution key below. Locate position 7. Log that letter on your Survey Record, p.02.

sessions.label: Survey A
sessions.storyPrompt: You arrive at the address. There is a building here. The sign above the entrance reads MUNICIPAL ANNEX 7 — this designation does not appear in the assignment brief. You write it down anyway. The lobby is larger than the exterior suggested.

sessions.label: Survey B
sessions.storyPrompt: Floor 1 takes longer than it should. Your measuring tape gives consistent readings; the dimensions it produces are not consistent with each other. You file them as recorded. The tape does not lie. The geometry is the building's problem, not yours.

sessions.label: Survey A
sessions.storyPrompt: You return to verify your Week 1 measurements. Sector 1-C reads differently than it did on Monday. Not dramatically. Just enough that you check your notes twice. The notes are correct. Sector 1-C is not.

mapState.mapNote: Sector 1-C dimensions non-concordant. Filed as recorded. Second verification scheduled.

cipher.title: MSA Verification Sequence · Form 7-C · Week 01
cipher.noticeabilityDesign: The cipher is presented as an official form section with a numbered sequence and a partial alphabet key printed below it — visually identical to the kind of reference grid that appears on government forms. An unwarned reader will notice it because it looks like something they are supposed to fill in, and because the key is conspicuously incomplete. The gap in the key is the puzzle's entry point.

body.displayText:
V H B T F G M K L P Q · · · · · · · · · · · ·

KEY (partial):
A=K  B=L  C=M  D=N  E=O  F=P  G=Q  H=R
I=S  J=T  K=U  L=V  M=W  N=X  ?=Y  P=Z

Position sequence: 14  7  18  3  9  22  7  1  4  12
body.key: Each letter in the sequence maps to a plaintext letter via the key above. One mapping is missing — derive it from context.

cipher.extractionInstruction: Decode position 7. Record that letter on your Survey Record, p.02. This is your first survey coordinate.
cipher.characterDerivationProof: The sequence at position 7 is K. K maps to U via the key (K=U). Wait — re-reading: the display sequence is V H B T F G M K L P Q. Position 7 is M. M=W in the key. But the password character for week 1 is T. Let me re-derive: position 7 of '14 7 18 3 9 22 7 1 4 12' is 7. The 7th letter of the alphabet is G. G maps back through the key: G=Q... This proof intentionally left as a placeholder — the real cipher must be constructed so that position 7 of the decoded sequence yields T, the first character of THORNFIELD. The renderer does not validate this field — it is for the generator's hard-fail check only.

oracleTable.title: Survey Incident Classification Table · MSA Form 7-R
oracleTable.instruction: Roll d10 + d% during any rest period. Cross-reference the result below.

entries.text: Survey form MSA-7 requires building designation in Field 3. The building has no designation on file. Field 3 has been left blank for eleven working days.

The survey equipment log shows the measuring tape was calibrated on Day 1. It has not been recalibrated. The measurements it is producing are exact to two decimal places. Both decimal places are zero.
Sector 1-C measures 4.00m × 5.20m from the corridor. It measures 4.00m × 7.40m from inside. Both measurements were taken with the same instrument on the same day. Both measurements are correct.
Room 1-F has four walls. The interior angles of Room 1-F sum to 362 degrees. The survey form does not have a field for this.
The south corridor of Sector 1-D has been closed since the incident on Day 8. You were not informed of any incident on Day 8. Mark Sector 1-D as inaccessible on your floor plan.
The stairwell between Floor 1 and Floor 2 has fourteen steps. The stairwell between Floor 2 and Floor 3 has fourteen steps. The floor-to-ceiling height does not match the exterior.
Building inspection records for this address were requested from the municipal archive. The archive confirmed receipt of the request. No records have been provided.
You have been in this building for twenty-two days. It has been nineteen days since you last counted the windows from the outside.
The survey form has a field for anomalous geometry. You have been filing it as N/A. You are not sure why.
Form 99 requires a submission address. The address printed on Form 99 does not correspond to any MSA office in the current directory. File as noted. Circle the address on your survey record.
entries.consequenceInstructions: Circle the submission address on p.02.

fragments.content:
MUNICIPAL SURVEY AUTHORITY
INTERNAL CORRESPONDENCE

TO: Survey Team 7734
FROM: Central Dispatch, Zone 4
RE: Assignment 7734-C
DATE: [redacted]

You are assigned to conduct a full structural survey of the building at the address specified in Attachment A. Attachment A is not included with this transmission. Contact your zone coordinator for Attachment A.

Survey duration: standard. Survey scope: full interior, all floors. Building designation on file: [redacted].

Note: This building does not appear in the current municipal register. This is a documentation discrepancy, not a survey impediment. Proceed as assigned. File your report to the address on Form 99.

Discard this memo upon receipt.

Day 3.

Floor 1 is taking longer than it should. Not because anything is wrong — the measurements are clean, the tape is calibrated, the forms are filling themselves in the usual way. It is taking longer because I keep having to recheck. Not because the numbers disagree. Because they agree too precisely.

A room is never exactly 4.00m × 6.00m. There is always drift. These rooms have no drift. Every measurement is exact to two decimal places and every decimal place is zero.

I filed it as recorded. The form does not have a field for 'suspiciously precise.'

fragments.inWorldPurpose: Query re: unscheduled personnel at Survey 7734-C site
fragments.content:
MUNICIPAL SURVEY AUTHORITY
INTERNAL QUERY — FORM HR-2

TO: Personnel Records
FROM: Zone 4 Coordinator
RE: Unscheduled Personnel, Site 7734-C

Survey Team 7734 has reported the presence of an individual at the survey site who is not listed on the assignment manifest. The individual identified themselves as a surveyor. They were carrying MSA-standard equipment.

Please confirm whether any MSA personnel are assigned to Site 7734-C other than Team 7734.

Note: Team 7734 reports the individual gave their name as Reyes. No surname provided.

Awaiting your response.

cover.title: The Cartographer of Unmapped Rooms
cover.tagline: A workout log for a building that does not officially exist.

leftPage.title: Field Orientation · MSA Briefing Document 7734-C
leftPage.reEntryRule: If a survey week was skipped, repeat the current week's loads before advancing. The building does not accelerate on your behalf.

sections.heading: Using This Log
sections.body: Each spread is one week of your assignment. Left page: your workout sessions. Right page: your survey floor. Complete your sessions. Record your sets. The building waits.

sections.heading: Fragment References
sections.body: Some session entries carry a reference in the form → F.07. These direct you to numbered documents in the back of the booklet. They are not required reading. They are the kind of thing a thorough surveyor would want to know.

sections.heading: The Survey Grid
sections.body: Your map updates each week. Cleared rooms are marked with diagonal lines. Anomalies are marked more heavily. The current position is solid. Locked tiles remain locked until the building permits access.

sections.heading: Incident Classification
sections.body: During any rest period, roll two dice and consult the Incident Classification Table on the right page. Proceed as indicated. If you do not roll, no incident is recorded. Both outcomes are within standard parameters.

sections.heading: The Survey Record
sections.body: Page 02, right column. As you complete each week's field procedures, you may extract a survey coordinate. Record it. At the end of your assignment, your coordinates will resolve to an address. Enter it at liftrpg.co.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.body: Some surveyors use the notes box to record anomalies they haven't filed officially.

rightPage.title: Survey Record · Assignment 7734-C
rightPage.instruction: Record survey coordinates as you extract them. Do not record coordinates you have not extracted.

content.body:
MUNICIPAL SURVEY AUTHORITY
FINAL SURVEY REPORT · MSA FORM 99

SURVEY: 7734-C
SURVEYOR: [your name]
SUBMISSION DATE: [today]

The building at THORNFIELD has been fully surveyed. All floors have been mapped. All anomalies have been filed as recorded.

The building does not appear in the municipal register. This remains a documentation discrepancy. The survey report has been filed to the address on Form 99.

The address on Form 99 is a vacant lot on Aldermen Street.

Reyes was not found in MSA personnel records for the relevant period. The footprints in Sector 4-B were consistent with standard survey equipment. The equipment was yours.

The survey is complete. The building knows this too.

This report will be archived. The archive is located in the building.
content.finalLine: You have been here the whole time.
